title: The Body Keeps the Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
core_idea: Trauma reshapes both brain and body, and healing requires addressing both. Van der Kolk synthesizes decades of research to show how trauma manifests physically and offers paths to recovery through body-based therapies.
key_concepts: Trauma is stored in the body, not just the mind.
The brain's fear response can become stuck in 'on' mode after trauma.
Traditional talk therapy alone is often insufficient for trauma recovery.
Body-based approaches (yoga, EMDR, neurofeedback) can rewire the trauma response.
Safety and connection are prerequisites for healing.
notable_quote: As long as you keep secrets and suppress information, you are fundamentally at war with yourself.
practical_lessons: Movement and body awareness are powerful tools for healing trauma.
Relationships and community are essential for recovery.
Understanding your triggers can help you regain control.
Healing is possible at any age — the brain remains plastic.
